description: An access-master record for a job seniority level. There are 7 ranks; the rank drives the 'same-or-higher rank' eligibility check for assignment and reassignment (axiom A5) and a sufficiently senior assignee acting as approver. A designation can also carry permissions, one of the four RBAC grant sources. A user references one designation.

fields.name: designationId
fields.label: Designation No.
fields.purpose: Stable key a user's designationId foreign-keys to so a designation can be renamed or re-ranked without re-keying every user.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Designation Name
fields.validation: non-blank; unique; e.g. Officer, Manager, Head, Director; the display field for the designation reference
fields.purpose: The title shown in the user form's designation dropdown; the label an admin recognizes when setting a user's seniority.

fields.name: rank
fields.label: Rank
fields.validation: >= 1; one of the 7 levels; higher number = more senior; used in same-or-higher comparisons
fields.purpose: The numeric seniority the assignment/reassignment eligibility check reads ('assigner rank >= the stage's required rank', a sufficiently senior assignee may approve); the single field axiom A5's rank guard compares.

fields.name: permissions
fields.label: Permissions
fields.validation: namespaced permission values granted to holders of this designation; one of the four grant sources unioned into the effective set
fields.purpose: Permissions every holder of the designation gets regardless of role (the designation grant source); deduplicated into the user's effective set alongside role, user-grant, and business-unit permissions.

invariants: A5 rank drives assignment/reassignment eligibility (assigner rank >= required); an APPROVED stage cannot be reassigned
A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
7 ranks; permissions are one of the four RBAC grant sources
